title: Add available-skill eval mode
description: Add a naturalistic eval mode where project skills are available in the isolated Goose environment but not forcibly injected into the prompt. Compare forced-vs-none, available-vs-none, and forced-vs-available to separate skill content quality from skill discoverability/selection quality.
acceptance_criteria: Runner supports available-skill configurations; no full SKILL.md injection in available mode; isolated Goose home exposes project skills; artifacts record available/expected/loaded skills and selection misses; docs explain forced vs available vs none modes; subset eval proves behavior.
close_reason: Config.inject_mode field (inject/available/none), copy_minimal_goose_config installs skills into isolated HOME for available mode, build_eval_prompt returns discovery-mode message, prepare_configs supports with-without-available, metadata records inject_mode and available_skills.

title: Add systematic debugging skill candidate
close_reason: Installed the strongest candidate external skill, obra/superpowers@systematic-debugging, as a project-local skill for future harness debugging/eval-analysis work.

title: Render escaped newlines in eval review code blocks
close_reason: Decoded escaped newline/tab sequences inside Markdown-rendered review code blocks so JSON command/stdout payloads display with real visual line breaks.

title: Fix eval review Markdown code block contrast
close_reason: Fixed Markdown-rendered code block contrast in eval review HTML and patched existing generated reviews.

title: Reduce agentic-dev-harness max-turn saturation
description: Latest suite shows agentic-dev-harness positive delta (+0.70) but all scenarios max-turn hit. Add stronger stop rules or runner prompts and verify handoff completeness without 100-turn exhaustion.
close_reason: max_turns reduced 100→40/25/40 per difficulty. Stop-rule expected_behavior items added for normal and difficult scenarios.

title: Rework goose-orchestration evals for positive discriminating signal
description: Latest suite has goose-orchestration with_skill=0.7778, without_skill=1.0 and both hit max turns. Rework scenarios and/or skill stop rules to test orchestration-specific value without over-guiding baseline.
close_reason: All 3 scenarios now require explicit Orchestration decision + Delegation audit labels that baseline won't produce. max_turns reduced to 20/25/30.

title: Make code-review eval fixtures more discriminant
description: Latest suite has code-review with_skill=without_skill=100%. Create normal/difficult/very_difficult fixtures that remain coherent with code-review but reduce answer-key leakage and baseline obviousness.
close_reason: Removed query answer-key hints, neutralised all fixture_descriptions, added verdict + Beads bd create requirements to expected_behavior across all 3 difficulty tiers.

title: Add eval efficiency gate and max-turn quality scoring
description: Turn pass=1.0 plus max_turns_reached into an explicit quality warning/gate. Add analysis --check thresholds for max-turn hit rate, neutral/negative deltas, and efficiency failures.
close_reason: Added --check gate, --max-turn-threshold, --negative-delta-gate, --efficiency-gate to analyze script. Model errors surfaced in analysis tables. Difficulty column added.

title: Clean up harness-review recipe: remove redundant topic param and tighten instructions
description: harness-review.yaml sub_recipes values block passes topic: "{{ task }}" to the harness_review subrecipe, but the subrecipe only accepts task, repo_path, and constraints. The extra topic key is silently ignored. Also the instructions text says 'Load the agentic-dev-harness skill' without specifying how, which is vague given the skills extension is already bundled.
acceptance_criteria: topic field removed from sub_recipes values; instructions clarified to reflect skills extension usage; recipe validates cleanly
close_reason: Removed redundant topic param; clarified skills extension instructions. Recipe validates cleanly.

title: ui-quality: intégrer Atomic Design — jwilger/agent-skills@atomic-design (CC0)
description:
Source: https://skills.sh/jwilger/agent-skills/atomic-design (181 installs, CC0-1.0, v1.2.1)
npx skills add jwilger/agent-skills@atomic-design

Ajouter dans ui-quality/references/ la hiérarchie atomique :
- Atoms (≤50 lignes, un élément visuel, tokens pour toutes propriétés)
- Molecules (1 interaction pattern, compose des atoms)
- Organisms (une feature area, compose molecules+atoms)
- Templates (structure page, pas de données spécifiques)
Règle clé: never skip a level, composants présentationnels (data via props, pas de fetch).
Parfait pour évaluer la cohérence structurelle du design system.
close_reason: Atomic Design hierarchy table ajouté dans ui-quality/SKILL.md avec progressive disclosure

title: KG-08: MCP App visualiseur Knowledge Graph (Goose Apps)
description:
Activer extension apps builtin (Goose 1.37) + visualiseur KG en Cytoscape.js.

FRAMEWORK RECOMMANDÉ: Cytoscape.js v3.34 + cytoscape-fcose
  Raisons: sémantique native (node types, edge labels), fcose layout >> d3-force,
  embed 2 CDN scripts, 13 open issues (meilleure maintenance), 43M npm/month.
  Voir docs/kg-visualization-framework-research.md pour analyse complète.

ALTERNATIVE si KG >500 nœuds: Sigma.js v3 + Graphology (WebGL, ForceAtlas2).

PLAN:
1. Activer: apps: enabled:true dans config
2. Réécrire scripts/kg-visualizer.html avec Cytoscape.js + fcose (2-4h)
3. Demander à Goose create_app avec le HTML généré
4. Tester sandbox access à .knowledge/memory.jsonl via fetch()

Ref: docs/kg-visualization-framework-research.md
close_reason: KG-08 implémenté: apps builtin enabled (apps:true), kg-visualizer MCP enabled dans config.yaml, apps/kg-visualizer-app/ créé avec HTML standalone. Deux modes: MCP server (show_kg_visualizer) + apps builtin HTML.

title: KG-08: MCP App pour visualiser le Knowledge Graph (Goose Apps extension)
description:
Créer une Goose App (MCP) pour visualiser .knowledge/memory.jsonl directement dans Goose.

Référence: https://goose-docs.ai/docs/tutorials/building-mcp-apps/
Goose Apps = extensions HTML/CSS/JS qui tournent dans des fenêtres sandboxées.

Avantages vs solutions actuelles:
  dist/kg/index.html (D3.js standalone) — nécessite xdg-open, pas dans Goose
  memory-visualizer React (third-parties/) — nécessite npm run dev + browser
  memviz.herich.tech (online) — nécessite connexion internet + upload manuel

MCP App = visualiseur intégré dans Goose, accessible via slash command ou sidebar.

Ce qu'il faudrait implémenter:
  - App HTML/JS qui lit memory.jsonl via un tool MCP (read_graph ou read_file)
  - Force-directed graph D3.js (réutiliser scripts/kg-visualizer.html comme base)
  - Accès au KG courant sans drag & drop manuel
  - Extension type: apps dans goose config
  
Investigation préalable:
  - Lire docs/tutorial building-mcp-apps
  - Vérifier si l'extension apps peut accéder aux fichiers locaux
  - Évaluer si on peut passer le chemin .knowledge/memory.jsonl dynamiquement
  - Comparer avec activation de l'extension autovisualiser (déjà dans config, disabled)

Lien avec bead KG-07 (memory builtin) et KG visualizer existant.
close_reason: Doublon de KG-08 — fermé.

title: KG-07: memory builtin - taxonomie categories SDD + integration recettes/agents
description:
Integration du memory builtin Goose dans le workflow SDD.

Categories (.goose/memory/<cat>.txt):
  harness/agents    = agents + roles
  harness/recipes   = recettes + slash commands
  product/features  = features en cours + status
  sdd/phase         = phase courante + bead + AC IDs
  sdd/gaps          = ACs sans test, features sans story

Integration:
  discover.yaml: remember_memory product/features apres epic cree
  implement.yaml: remember_memory sdd/phase a chaque GREEN phase
  review.yaml: remember_memory sdd/gaps si AC sans test detecte

Contrainte: chaque categorie < 500 chars (auto-injecte dans chaque prompt).
Difference vs bd remember: memory = session context; bd remember = faits durables git-synced.

title: ux-quality: investiguer phazurlabs/agentic-ai-generative-ux — UX des systèmes agentiques
description:
Source: https://github.com/phazurlabs/ux-ui-mastery/tree/main/skills/agentic-ai-generative-ux
Références: agentic-multi-agent-patterns.md, ai-native-interface-patterns.md, ai-safety-trust-guardrails.md, llm-hallucination-design-guardrails.md

Très pertinent pour notre propre harness: nos utilisateurs sont des utilisateurs d'une UI agentique.
- Trust calibration pour les agents IA
- Patterns multi-agent orchestration
- Guardrails de sécurité UX
- Paradigme outils→agents (Gartner #1 trend 2025)
À investiguer pour une future skill agentic-ux ou comme référence dans ux-quality.

title: ux-quality: intégrer phazurlabs/design-critique-case-studies — méthodologie
description:
Source: https://github.com/phazurlabs/ux-ui-mastery/tree/main/skills/design-critique-case-studies
Références: critique-methodology.md, redesign-failure-analysis.md, product-deep-dives.md

Enrichir ux-quality avec méthodologie de critique structurée:
- Jobs-to-be-Done framework
- Principes Gestalt appliqués
- Heuristiques d'utilisabilité (Nielsen 10)
- Analyse de redesigns qui ont échoué
Complémente notre dimension 4 (design coherence).

title: ui-quality: investiguer thebushidocollective/han@atomic-design-fundamentals (500 installs)
description:
npx skills add thebushidocollective/han@atomic-design-fundamentals
Le plus populaire sur atomic design (500 installs vs 181 pour jwilger).
Contenu non accessible en raw — nécessite installation.
A 5 skills dans la série: fundamentals, integration, templates, atoms, (d'autres).
À comparer avec jwilger/atomic-design avant de choisir lequel intégrer.

title: ux-quality: evaluate vasilyu1983/ai-agents-public@software-ux-research (153 installs)
description:
npx skills add vasilyu1983/ai-agents-public@software-ux-research
Read content, extract UX research methods (personas, journey maps, usability testing scripts) to enrich ux-quality.
Also evaluate: absolutelyskilled/absolutelyskilled@ux-research (151), rampstackco/claude-skills@ux-research (97).

title: Eval: store Goose session ID — switch from --no-session to named sessions
description:
Currently eval runs use --no-session (ephemeral, no session ID persisted).
We added first_message_id (Anthropic API trace) + token counts as practical alternatives.

To get a real Goose session ID for post-hoc inspection:
1. Replace --no-session with --session eval-{run_id}-{eval_id}-{config}-{run_num}
2. After the run, read the session ID from the goose-home sessions directory
3. Store it in eval_run_results.goose_session_id
4. The session file at ~/.local/state/goose/sessions/ (or isolated goose-home) would then be inspectable with 'goose session list' and 'goose session inspect <id>'

Tradeoff: named sessions persist to disk (more storage), but enable full post-hoc conversation replay.
Current workaround: events.jsonl + first_message_id + token counts cover most debugging needs.

value: Claude Agent Skills platform: canonical source is https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/agent-skills/overview; read when building or debugging skills; invariant: skills = SKILL.md + optional references/ scripts/ assets/; allowed-tools field is experimental.

Harness agents: 11 named agents in .agents/agents/ (architect, product-owner, beads-planner, codebase-researcher, tdd-guide, implementation-worker, review-critic, principal-engineer, qa-automation, ui-ux-auditor, harness-orchestrator); entry point for multi-agent work is harness-orchestrator; descriptions are the routing signals.

DORA env labels: env:dev (implemented locally), env:staging (deployed to staging), env:prod (deployed to production). Apply via bd update <id> --add-label env:prod. Required to measure lead-time and change-failure-rate.
DORA issue types: task (feature), bug (fix), incident (prod outage). Label with severity:low/medium/high/critical and env:dev/staging/prod.

Goose adversary inspector: create ~/.config/goose/adversary.md to enable argument-level shell blocking (LLM-based); blocks sudo/privilege-escalation while allowing all normal dev commands; activated by file presence; disabled if file absent.

Goose custom agents spec: canonical source is https://goose-docs.ai/docs/guides/context-engineering/custom-agents/; frontmatter supports name(required), description, model; @mention, natural-language, and delegate() invocation patterns all work.

Harness routing: canonical source is .agents/skills/goose-orchestration/SKILL.md#Agent and Subrecipe Routing Table; load goose-orchestration skill when delegating to any agent or subrecipe; invariant: always call load() before delegate() to confirm live availability.
KG viz framework: Cytoscape.js v3.34+fcose recommandé (sémantique native, 13 issues, fcose layout); Sigma.js v3+Graphology si >500 nœuds (WebGL). Voir docs/kg-visualization-framework-research.md

SOTA knowledge base (July 2026): canonical source is docs/sota-knowledge-base.md; read when designing skills/agents/loops or debugging eval failures; invariant: check gaps table before writing new skill instructions.
DORA lead-time: measured from bead created_at (intent) to env:prod label added (deployed). Change-failure-rate: bugs/incidents opened after env:prod label / total env:prod deployments.

Harness entry: /harness slash command = harness-master recipe; auto-discovers agents via load(); use for any task you cannot route immediately; load goose-orchestration skill for routing table before first delegate() call of a session.
